channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The WordPress drama could have major implications for the internet
item.description: The WordPress situation has soured in the past several weeks, as a dispute spirals out of control between founder Matt Mullenweg and WP Engine, a platform that many users deploy in order to host their WordPress sites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Six steps to creating a successful IT strategy: A guide for CIOs
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, the role of the CIO has transcended simply managing IT infrastructure to becoming a pivotal player in enabling business strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cyber criminals are increasingly helping Russia and China target the US and allies, Microsoft says
item.description: Russia, China and Iran are expanding their partnerships with cyber criminals to conduct cyberespionage and cyberattacks against the U.S. and other nations, according to a new report from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Avaya helped Leon County rapidly deploy a contact center during a state of emergency
item.description: In May 2024, Leon County, Florida faced many significant challenges when three tornadoes, including two F2s and one F1, struck the area causing extensive damage. This natural disaster resulted in 725 severely damaged structures, 175 of which were destroyed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biden admin to provide $750 million to North Carolina-based Wolfspeed for advanced computer chips
item.description: The Biden-Harris administration announced plans to provide up to $750 million in direct funding to Wolfspeed, with the money supporting its new silicon carbide factory in North Carolina that makes the wafers used in advanced computer chips and its factory in Marcy, New York.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cloud Security Worsening Despite Better Tools: Report
item.description: Cloud security vulnerabilities rising despite better tools, exposing sensitive data and making organizations at risk of cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Predict the next unicorn? Insights from “superforecaster” VCs can help. Here’s how
item.description: Machines outperform humans in countless ways—crunching data, organizing information, finding patterns, and even generating ideas. Yet there’s one crucial skill where we still hold the edge: predicting the future.
item.dc:creator: JP Mark, Fast Company

item.title: Open source package entry points could be used for command jacking
item.description: Open source application packages, including those in Python and JavaScript, have a vulnerability in their entry points that could be used by threat actors to execute malicious code to steal data, plant malware, and more.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: AI being used to run world’s first indoor vertical berry farm
item.description: First indoor vertical berry farm opens in Virginia, using AI to grow 4 million pounds annually. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a closer look at the "growing" trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top 5 methods to equip your car with Wi-Fi
item.description: Having Wi-Fi has become a necessity for many of us. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the top five ways to equip your vehicles with Wi-Fi.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: AI is killing cloud sustainability
item.description: Sustainability was once a highly touted advantage in public cloud computing. Enterprises and cloud providers flaunted their green initiatives, promoting data centers powered by renewable energy that would reduce carbon footprints. This topic has quietly slipped off the radar in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Say goodbye to dead batteries with this portable wind turbine
item.description: The Shine 2.0 is a 3-in-1 portable wind turbine, charge controller and power bank that fits into a backpack and works day and night, rain or shine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft previews AI ‘building blocks’ for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing packages geared to integrating generative AI services into .NET applications. Called Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions and Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries, the packages are available in preview as of October 8.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
